learning.title: Learn one useful step
learning.prompt: Pick one concept you can understand in five minutes and write the takeaway.

learning.title: Explain it simply
learning.prompt: Choose one idea and explain it in three plain sentences.
learning.why: Teaching the idea back makes it stick.

learning.title: Skill micro-drill
learning.prompt: Practice one small skill rep without opening another feed.
learning.why: Small reps build competence without needing novelty.

learning.title: Question then answer
learning.prompt: Write one question you genuinely want answered and find the shortest local note or book passage that helps.
learning.why: Curiosity is healthier than compulsive scanning.

learning.title: Save a useful lesson
learning.prompt: Capture one durable lesson in your own words.
learning.why: A saved lesson gives the attention loop a constructive endpoint.

creating.title: Make one small artifact
creating.prompt: Draft one paragraph, sketch, outline, hook, or prototype improvement.
creating.why: Creating turns energy into something durable.

creating.title: Revise one thing
creating.prompt: Open an existing draft and improve one sentence, frame, or detail.
creating.why: Revision is a low-friction way back into creation.

creating.title: Capture the next idea
creating.prompt: Write one idea you can build later, then close the loop.
creating.why: Capture prevents ideas from turning into endless browsing.

creating.title: Ship a tiny version
creating.prompt: Make the smallest publishable version of the thing you are avoiding.

creating.title: Constraint sprint
creating.prompt: Create something using only what is already open or available.

working.title: Close one loop
working.prompt: Choose a visible task and move it forward for ten focused minutes.

working.title: Send the message
working.prompt: Send or draft the practical message you have been delaying.
working.why: One closed communication loop can free attention.

working.title: Clear the next step
working.prompt: Write the next action for your most important project.
working.why: Clarity is a healthy replacement for restless checking.

working.title: Focused block
working.prompt: Set a 15-minute timer and work on the task already chosen.
working.why: Time-boxing keeps effort bounded and non-addictive.

working.title: Tidy the workspace
working.prompt: Remove one source of friction from your physical or digital workspace.
working.why: A clearer environment supports calmer attention.

fitness.title: Move before more media
fitness.prompt: Walk, stretch, or do one low-friction movement set.
fitness.why: Movement helps reset attention and mood.

fitness.title: Three-minute reset
fitness.prompt: Do three minutes of mobility or bodyweight movement.
fitness.why: Short movement interrupts stuck scrolling without overwhelm.

fitness.title: Hydrate and stand
fitness.prompt: Drink water and stand away from the screen for one minute.
fitness.why: A physical state change makes the next choice easier.

fitness.title: Walk outside
fitness.prompt: Take a short walk and name five things you can see.
fitness.why: Outdoor cues pull attention back into the real world.

fitness.title: Prepare the next workout
fitness.prompt: Set out shoes, clothes, or the first exercise for later.
fitness.why: Preparation lowers the barrier to healthy action.

calm.title: Settle the nervous system
calm.prompt: Take six slow breaths and relax your shoulders before continuing.
calm.why: Calm gives you more choice over the next click.

calm.title: Name what is happening
calm.prompt: Say: I am noticing an urge to keep scrolling, and I can choose the next action.
calm.why: Naming the loop weakens it without shame.

calm.title: Look away reset
calm.prompt: Look at something across the room and unclench your jaw.
calm.why: Visual distance helps break screen intensity.

calm.title: One quiet minute
calm.prompt: Set the device down and take one quiet minute.
calm.why: Stillness creates space between impulse and action.

calm.title: Slow exhale
calm.prompt: Breathe in normally and make the exhale longer for five rounds.
calm.why: Longer exhales can support calm attention.

family.title: Strengthen one bond
family.prompt: Send a specific kind check-in to someone close to you.
family.why: Healthy connection competes with empty scrolling.

family.title: Plan one helpful thing
family.prompt: Choose one small action that makes home life easier today.

family.title: Ask a better question
family.prompt: Ask someone close: what would help today?

family.title: Share appreciation
family.prompt: Name one specific thing you appreciate about a family member.

family.title: Schedule real time
family.prompt: Put one family or close-relationship block on the calendar.

spirituality.title: Pause for grounding
spirituality.prompt: Take a short prayer, gratitude, or reflection pause.
spirituality.why: Spiritual grounding can restore perspective without forcing positivity.

spirituality.title: Recall one line
spirituality.prompt: Bring to mind one line of wisdom, scripture, or reflection that steadies you.
spirituality.why: A remembered line can interrupt reactive attention.

spirituality.title: Practice patience
spirituality.prompt: Choose one patient response you can make today.
spirituality.why: Spiritual practice becomes real through action.

spirituality.title: Gratitude prayer
spirituality.prompt: Name one burden and one gift, then release the next click.
spirituality.why: This holds hard reality and hope together.

spirituality.title: Serve quietly
spirituality.prompt: Do one quiet helpful act without announcing it.
spirituality.why: Service moves attention from performance to purpose.

business_progress.title: Move a real offer forward
business_progress.prompt: Write the next business decision, client action, invoice step, or launch task.
business_progress.why: Business progress redirects urgency into useful action.

business_progress.title: Verification habit
business_progress.prompt: Before considering any money claim, write the source, incentive, and proof you would need.
business_progress.why: Verification protects attention and finances from hype.

business_progress.title: Revenue reality check
business_progress.prompt: Identify one real customer problem you can help solve today.
business_progress.why: Real business progress is grounded in service, not hype.

business_progress.title: Send one useful follow-up
business_progress.prompt: Follow up with one client, lead, collaborator, or invoice.
business_progress.why: A real follow-up beats speculative finance content.

business_progress.title: Define the offer
business_progress.prompt: Write your offer in one clear sentence.
business_progress.why: Clarity is better than chasing get-rich signals.

emotional_regulation.title: Name and lower the intensity
emotional_regulation.prompt: Write one sentence: what happened, what I feel, what I will do next.
emotional_regulation.why: Naming emotion reduces the pull of reactive loops.

emotional_regulation.title: Delay the reaction
emotional_regulation.prompt: Wait two minutes before responding or clicking further.

emotional_regulation.title: Separate fact from story
emotional_regulation.prompt: Write one fact and one interpretation you are adding.
emotional_regulation.why: This lowers the grip of outrage and shame.

emotional_regulation.title: Choose the next useful action
emotional_regulation.prompt: Pick one small action that helps Future You.
emotional_regulation.why: Action gives emotion a healthy channel.

emotional_regulation.title: Body check
emotional_regulation.prompt: Notice jaw, shoulders, breath, and hands; soften one area.
emotional_regulation.why: Physical awareness can reduce reactive intensity.

positive_history.title: Learn one story of resilience
positive_history.prompt: Read about someone who overcame hardship and write one lesson you can use today.
positive_history.why: Positive history builds perspective without doomscrolling.

positive_history.title: Inventor lesson
positive_history.prompt: Study one inventor or builder and note the repeated habit behind their work.
positive_history.why: Useful history points toward practice, not passive awe.

positive_history.title: Recovery story
positive_history.prompt: Find one story of rebuilding after loss and write the first practical step they took.
positive_history.why: Recovery stories make hardship concrete without helplessness.

positive_history.title: Civil courage
positive_history.prompt: Read a short account of civil courage and write the value it required.
positive_history.why: Courage history can strengthen civic awareness without ragebait.

positive_history.title: Ancestor perspective
positive_history.prompt: Write one hardship your ancestors or elders faced and one strength you can carry forward.
positive_history.why: Perspective can reduce panic and increase responsibility.

constructive_news.title: Turn news into one useful action
constructive_news.prompt: Read the factual update, identify one practical next step, then stop scanning.
constructive_news.why: Constructive news keeps awareness without helplessness framing.

constructive_news.title: Find the resource
constructive_news.prompt: Look for the official resource, safety step, or community response in the story.
constructive_news.why: Resources matter more than repeated alarm.

constructive_news.title: Action boundary
constructive_news.prompt: Decide whether this news asks for action, prayer, preparation, or simply awareness.
constructive_news.why: Boundaries keep news from becoming doomscrolling.

constructive_news.title: One update only
constructive_news.prompt: Read one factual update and then return to your chosen task.
constructive_news.why: A single update protects awareness without feeding compulsion.

constructive_news.title: Help locally
constructive_news.prompt: If the issue is local, write one grounded way to help or prepare.
constructive_news.why: Real-world action converts concern into agency.

civic_awareness.title: Use civic information cleanly
civic_awareness.prompt: Separate facts from framing and note whether there is a local action to take.
civic_awareness.why: Civic awareness is useful when it does not become an outrage loop.

civic_awareness.title: Check the meeting
civic_awareness.prompt: Identify one relevant meeting, vote, deadline, or public notice.
civic_awareness.why: Civic action is healthier than outrage cycling.

civic_awareness.title: Name the decision-maker
civic_awareness.prompt: Write who can actually act on the issue.

civic_awareness.title: One respectful message
civic_awareness.prompt: Draft a clear, respectful civic comment or question if action is warranted.

civic_awareness.title: Civic closeout
civic_awareness.prompt: Write what you learned, what you can do, and when to stop reading.
civic_awareness.why: A closeout prevents civic awareness from becoming a loop.

gratitude.title: Notice what is still good
gratitude.prompt: Name three concrete things that are working today.
gratitude.why: Gratitude balances attention without denying hard reality.

gratitude.title: Good evidence
gratitude.prompt: Write one piece of evidence that your life is not only this feed.

gratitude.title: Small beauty
gratitude.prompt: Look for one beautiful or orderly thing nearby.
gratitude.why: Attention can be trained toward what is life-giving.

gratitude.title: Gratitude and action
gratitude.prompt: Name one gift and one action that honors it.

discipline.title: Choose the next right action
discipline.prompt: Do the smallest disciplined action available for five minutes.
discipline.why: Discipline converts distraction energy into agency.

discipline.title: Phone down rule
discipline.prompt: Put the device down until one real-world action is complete.
discipline.why: A simple rule is easier than negotiating with every impulse.

discipline.title: Verify before believing
discipline.prompt: For any exciting claim, write what proof would change your mind.

discipline.title: One tiny promise
discipline.prompt: Make and keep one tiny promise to yourself right now.
discipline.why: Trust is rebuilt through kept promises.

discipline.title: Stop at enough
discipline.prompt: Decide the enough point before consuming more.

emergency_reset.title: Immediate reset
emergency_reset.prompt: Set the device down, breathe slowly six times, and stand up.
emergency_reset.why: A body reset interrupts compulsive loops quickly.

emergency_reset.title: Leave the feed
emergency_reset.prompt: Close the feed and open a real-world task already chosen.
emergency_reset.why: Changing context is often easier than winning a willpower contest.

emergency_reset.title: Name the loop
emergency_reset.prompt: Say: this is ragebait, doomscrolling, shame, or distraction; I can exit.
emergency_reset.why: Clear naming turns the loop into an object, not an identity.

emergency_reset.title: Reach out
emergency_reset.prompt: Message someone safe: I am stepping away from a bad loop.

emergency_reset.title: One grounding task
emergency_reset.prompt: Wash a dish, take out trash, walk outside, or tidy one surface.
